title: Catfish Springs’s “Southern Catfish Showdown” Food Challenge
description:
– This unique “Southern Catfish Showdown” challenge consists of 20 pieces of breaded catfish, 1lb of fries, 3 servings of cole slaw, 12 hush puppies, and a pitcher of beverage of your choice.
– There is a 30 minute time limit to finish the challenge. Winners get the meal for free, a sweet hat, $25 cash, a picture up on the wall of fame, and you get to sign a one dollar bill that will be displayed on the wall of fame.

title: Golden Rule BBQ’s “Big Pig” Pulled Pork Sandwich Challenge
description:
– This challenge consists of 2lbs of Golden Rule’s delicious pulled pork, 1lb of coleslaw, and 1lb of BBQ sauce all sandwiched between an oversized bun.
– In addition to the sandwich, there is a side of fries that must be consumed as part of the challenge.
– There is a 45 minute time limit to finish the entire sandwich and fries. Successful completions win the meal for free, a sweet t-shirt, and a photo on the wall of fame.

title: Scooter’s Sports Grill’s “Scooter” Burger Challenge
description:
– This burger challenge consists of four 1/2 lb burger patties, eight slices of bacon, four slices of cheese, four eggs (cooked your way), lettuce, tomatoes, onions, and pickles.
– In addition to the giant stacked burger, there are 2 lbs of fries and 1 lb of onion rings.
– There is a 30 minute time limit to finish the entire burger and sides. Winners get the meal for free!

title: Greasy Spoon Kitchen’s “Big Papa” Burger Challenge
where: Greasy Cove General Store & The Greasy Spoon Kitchen
description:
– The Greasy Spoon Kitchen is located inside the Greasy Cove General Store. The General Store was established in the 1940s and mainly sold groceries and medicine. Over the years the store added and changed but always remained a general store for the locals and surrounding areas.
– The burger challenge consists of a 5lb patty with 8 slices of American cheese, 5 onion rings, lettuce, tomato, onions, pickles, mayo, ketchup, and mustard. Everything is sandwiched between 8 regular sized burger buns.
– In addition to the burgers, there is 1 pound of fries.
– There is a 30 minute time limit to finish the entire platter. In addition, there is a 5 minute waiting period after the challenge is completed before a win is declared.
– Winners receive the meal for free, a $25 credit for future use, and a picture up on the wall of fame.

title: Fiesta Mexicana’s “Monster” Burrito Challenge (Florence)
description:
– There are many Fiesta Mexicana locations around Alabama, Tennessee, and Georgia, but not all of them offer this “Monster” Burrito Challenge. Contact the one nearest you to see if they offer the challenge there!!
– There is a one hour time limit to eat four ~1.5 pound burritos filled with egg, onions, bell peppers, lettuce, pico de gallo, rice, refried beans, grilled chicken, and shredded cheese, all wrapped inside a flour burrito tortilla and smothered with queso cheese dip sauce. The four burritos are placed on the outside of a large serving tray and then fries and carne asada steak meat are added into the middle. The whole platter weighs around 8-9 lbs total, and there is a one hour time limit to eat it all. Losers pay only $34.99 for the meal which is not a bad deal, considering how much food there is!! Winners get the entire meal free plus a sweet Fiesta Mexicana T-shirt, $100 in gift cards to come back and use later, and a spot on the restaurant location’s Wall of Fame!!

title: Johnny Brusco’s 6lb “Ultimate” Stromboli Challenge (Jasper)
where: Johnny Brusco's New York Style Pizza (Jasper)
description:
– This “Ultimate” Stromboli Challenge features a 6 lb (2.73 kg) stromboli with all the toppings featured on Johnny’s Deluxe Pizza. It includes sausage, onions, mushrooms, green peppers, ground beef, Canadian bacon, and pepperoni. There is also a lot of shredded mozzarella cheese. There is no pizza / red marinara sauce inside the stromboli. Rather, it is served on the side in a bowl which is required to be finished as part of the challenge within the 1 hour (60 minutes) time limit.
– Fail and pay $49.99 plus tax for your meal. Winners get the entire stromboli meal free plus a sweet t-shirt and spot on the location’s Wall of Fame.
– There are many Johnny’s (in Georgia) and Johnny Brusco’s restaurants all around the southeastern United States and most if not all of them can / will offer this stromboli challenge. Find and contact the location nearest you beforehand to set up your challenge so they can be prepared and ready for your challenge attempt!!
– CLICK HERE to check out the challenge terms and conditions on Johnny Brusco’s website!!

title: Jack Brown’s “Deep Fried Oreo” Challenge (Huntsville)
where: Jack Brown's Beer & Burger Joint Huntsville
address: 123 North Side Square Ste 100, Huntsville, AL 35801
description:
– This challenge consists of as many deep fried Double Stuf Oreo’s as you can eat.
– This challenge has a one-sitting time limit.
– To win, the current record must be beaten.
– Record resets at the beginning of every year; contact the Jack Brown’s location nearest you to find out the current record to beat!
– Winners receive the meal for free, their picture on the Wall of Fame and social media, and get to choose from either a T-Shirt, hat, hoodie, or sweatshirt, depending on the season.

title: Jack Brown’s “Hamburger” Challenge (Huntsville)
where: Jack Brown's Beer & Burger Joint Huntsville
address: 123 North Side Square Ste 100, Huntsville, AL 35801
description:
– This challenge consists of as many hamburgers as you can eat, each with a 4-ounce patty and a bun.
– This challenge has a one-sitting time limit.
– To win, the current record must be beaten.
– Record resets at the beginning of every year; contact the Jack Brown’s location nearest you to find out the current record to beat!
– Winners receive the meal for free, their picture on the Wall of Fame and social media, and get to choose from either a T-Shirt, hat, hoodie, or sweatshirt, depending on the season.

title: Sunliner Diner’s “Jukebox Burger” Challenge (Gulf Shores)
description:
– This pay-to-play challenge consists of a stack of three griddled burger patties, bacon, fried green tomato, onion ring, fried egg, fried pickles, and topped with American, Swiss, and cheddar cheese, drizzled with a creole hollandaise on a brioche bun, and a side of fries.
– One-sitting time limit.
– Order this burger and play any song of choice on the jukebox; finish the burger and winners will receive a “Sunliner” surprise.
Note: This challenge is not free or discounted if you win.

title: Dick’s Last Resort’s “Big Meat” Steak Challenge (Orange Beach)
address: 4720 Main Street, Suite M 101, Orange Beach, AL 36561
description:
– There are Dick’s Last Resort locations all around the country, and they all offer this “Big Meat” Challenge.
– The main feature of this challenge is two 12 oz New York strip steaks that are cooked to your liking.
– The steaks are the main feature of the challenge, but they aren’t really the hardest part to finish.
– Along with the steak, you must finish an order of their Macho Nachos which weighs over 3 lbs.
– The nachos are topped with cheese, homemade chili, Pico de Gallo, jalapenos, and sour cream.
– Another major feature of this challenge is Dick’s huge 12-inch footlong wiener hot dog loaded with chili, cheese, and onions. You must also finish a house salad, a double portion of their loaded red skin mashed potatoes (taters), and a double order of steamed broccoli. Good luck and win before you begin!!
– Winners receive the $79.99 meal free, a sweet t-shirt, and a spot on the location’s Wall of Fame.
– Please note that the drinks and dessert featured in the video below were just optional and not required.

title: Hickory Pit Too’s Boss Hawg Burger Challenge
13th Street BBQ’s 5lb “Hawg Dawg” Challenge
description:
– There is a giant 5 lb ” Hawg Dawg” topped with lettuce, tomatoes, red onion, and cheese.
– There is a 30-minute time limit to finish everything, served in a square metal tray.
– Click here to check out the restaurant’s website challenge page!!
– Free Meal
– Wall of Fame

title: Wacked Out Weiner’s Wackinator Hot Dog Challenge (Foley)
description:
– There are ~14 Wacked Out Weiner locations around the USA, with many of them in Alabama.
– Randy visited the Wacked Out Weiner location in Gulf Shores, Alabama, and everything was delicious!!
– There is 8 Nathan’s Famous hot dogs, totaling 1lb, inside a massive freshly baked bread loaf.
– The weiner hot dogs are topped with white mac and cheese, homemade chili, and baked beans.
– There is also sauerkraut, onions, some peppers, and a few other toppings. It was all ~5lbs total.
– There’s a 30 minute time limit. Winners get the $32.95 meal free, a sweet t-shirt, and Wall of Fame.
– Please note that other locations may have a different set of toppings on their Wackinator Challenge.
– 24-hour notice required.

title: Wacked Out Weiner’s Wackinator Hot Dog Challenge (Gulf Shores)
address: 3800 Gulf Shores Pkwy, Ste #340, Gulf Shores, AL 36542
description:
– There are ~14 Wacked Out Weiner locations around the USA, with many of them in Alabama.
– Randy visited the Wacked Out Weiner location in Gulf Shores, Alabama, and everything was delicious!!
– There is 8 Nathan’s Famous hot dogs, totaling 1lb, inside a massive freshly baked bread loaf.
– The weiner hot dogs are topped with white mac and cheese, homemade chili, and baked beans.

– Please note that other locations may have a different set of toppings on their Wackinator Challenge.
– 24-hour notice required.

title: El Olmeca Mexican Cantina’s “Burrito Macho XXL”
address: 7044 University Dr NW, Ste B, Huntsville, AL 35806
description:
– 6 lb burrito filled with chicken, steak, rice, beans, lettuce, and sour cream.
– Served wet and smothered with green and red sauces along with queso.
– There is a 20 minute time limit to finish the entire burrito meal.
– Time starts shortly after the burrito gets to your table so be ready.
– Free Meal
– $10 Gift Card to be used later
– Wall of Fame

title: Acme Oyster House’s 15 Dozen Oyster Club Challenge (Gulf Shores)
description:
– Acme Oyster House has 6 locations, all based in the southern US, and all offer this challenge.
– Each 1 dozen oysters is served on an individual tray (they vary in size).
– You must eat 15 dozen in less than 1 hour to win the challenge.
– You may attempt to break the house quantity record by 1 full dozen.
– You can eat them with a fork or use your hands and “slurp” them.
– Escorted restroom breaks are allowed, per the official rules.
– You should call ahead to make sure they have enough oysters.
– Free Hat and T-Shirt inside a special mesh gift bag.
– Name on location’s plaque or Wall Of Fame.
– You get 1/2 off all 15 dozen oysters (see below about required tip).
– If you break the location’s record, all of your oysters are free!! (some additional rules apply).
– NOTE: Regardless whether you win or lose, there is an 18% required tip for the shuckers.
– NOTE 2: If you want to film yourself, you must contact their management team for permission.

title: The Mugshots Burger Challenge (Vestavia Hills)
description:
– Three 1/2lb beef patties separated by layers of bread and cheese and toppings
– Served with 1lb of fries
– 12 minute time limit
– Free Meal
– Free T-shirt

title: The Mugshots Burger Challenge (Tuscaloosa)
description:
– Three 1/2lb beef patties separated by layers of bread and cheese and toppings
– Served with 1lb of fries
– 12 minute time limit
– Free Meal
– Free T-shirt

title: Rattlesnake Saloon’s “Gigantor” Burger Challenge
description:
– There is a giant beef patty that weighs over 2 lbs.
– It is topped with cheese, bacon, sauces, and healthy vegetables.
– The burger is surrounded by 1 lb of fries and 1 lb of onion rings.
– There is a 45 minute time limit to finish everything.
– Free Meal
– Free T-Shirt
– Wall Of Fame

title: Moose A’La Mode’s “Taste of Alaska” Food Challenge
description:
– For this challenge, one has to eat the different contents that include a hot dog, burger, and sandwich.
– This includes an Alaskan Reindeer Philly Sandwich, sixteen 1 oz Alaska Crab Cakes, Alaskan Crabby Patty Burger, Double Heart Attack Trophy Burger, and Alaskan Volcano Dog.
– Contestants must finish everything in 30 minutes and can consume drinks until the first bite of the hot dog.
– Winners receive the meal for free, free T-Shirt, and their picture on the Wall of Fame.

title: Prey Pub’s “Great Denali” Challenge
address: National Park and Preserve, 229 Parks Hwy, Denali Park, AK 99755
description:
– Challengers have 1 hour to finish the big meal.
– Winners receive the meal for free and a free t-shirt.

title: Flattop Pizza’s “Wild Thing” Alaskan Pizza Challenge
description: Flattop Pizza + Pool serves a delicious “Wild Thing” Pizza which is topped with wild Alaskan halibut (fish), yak meat, and mushrooms. Their largest pizza weighs around 6-8lbs and is cut up into 8 big slices. The challenge also includes a chopped salad, order of “Non-Alaskan” (Caribbean) Wings, and a root beer float for dessert. There is a 1 hour time limit to finish everything. Winners still must pay $100 for the meal but they receive a sweet t-shirt, a spot on the Wall of Fame, and bragging rights. Note: The meal unfortunately is not free if you finish.

title: West Rib Pub’s “Seward’s Folly” Caribou Burger Challenge
description: There are two 1lb caribou meat burger patties stacked and layered with 1lb deli-meat ham slices, 1/2lb bacon, two cheeses, some healthy vegetables, and lots of their delicious “Fat Sauce” (w/ mayonnaise and sour cream). The burger is served with 1.5lbs of battered fries and there is a 1 hour time limit to finish everything. Winners still must pay for the meal, but they win a sweet t-shirt and spot on the Wall of Fame.

title: Tiki Pete’s “Taste of Alaska” Food Challenge
description:
– For this challenge, one has to eat the different contents that include a hot dog, burger, and sandwich.
– This includes an Alaskan Reindeer Philly Sandwich, sixteen 1 oz Alaska Crab Cakes, Alaskan Crabby Patty Burger, Double Heart Attack Trophy Burger, and Alaskan Volcano Dog.
– Contestants must finish everything in 30 minutes and can consume drinks until the first bite of the hot dog.
– Free Meal
– Free T-Shirt
– Wall of Fame

title: Brewster’s Belly Buster Challenge (Teddy Bear Plaza)
description:
– There is a 3 pound burger plus 1 pound of fries and a 16oz beverage to be finished in 1 hour.
– There are 2 hamburger buns, 3 handcrafted 1lb beef patties, and 4 slices of Hickory bacon.
– The beef patties are stacked and layered with Swiss, Pepper Jack, and Cheddar cheeses.
– There is 4oz of homemade BBQ sauce, 4 tomato slices, 2 pieces of lettuce, and 4oz onions.
– The burger is topped with Chipotle or regular mayo and served with 1 lb of Brewster’s fries.
– You must get 16oz of soft drink. You can also get beer like I got, which was very delicious.
– There is a 1 hour time limit. Winners get the $40 meal free, sweet t-shirts, and Wall of Fame.
– This challenge is not offered on weekends.

title: Kriner’s Diner’s Family Burger Challenge (C Street)
description: There is a 2lb Certified Angus Beef burger patty topped with 8 slices of cheese, lettuce, tomato, onions, and you can get more toppings added if you want to. There is a big bun and the challenge includes 1lb of french fries. Winners receive the meal free, a sweet t-shirt, and their name and photo on the location’s Wall of Fame.

title: Humpy’s Great Alaskan “Kodiak Arrest” Crab Legs Challenge
description:
Humpy’s challenges you to eat 7 crab cakes/nuggets, 14 inches of reindeer sausage, 4 lbs of Alaskan King Crab Legs with side dishes, and their famous wild berry crisp with ice cream for dessert.
If you can put away all this food in 1 hour (without leaving the table!), you win a spot on their winners hall of fame and an “I got crabs at Humpy’s” t-shirt.  Oh yeah, & bragging rights!!
Note: The meal unfortunately is not free or discounted if you win.

title: Giligin’s “Big and Randy” Quadruple Stacked Pizza Challenge
address: 4251 N Winfield Scott Plaza, Scottsdale, AZ 85251
description:
– For this challenge, you must order FOUR (4) of Giligin’s homemade pizzas. They are baked, stacked, and topped with some Totino’s pizza rolls. The pizzas are served with an actual plunger filled with Giligin’s homemade ranch dressing, which is just optional. You are not required to finish every drop of the ranch dressing, or lick the plunger. There is a 30 minute time limit to finish all four pizzas.
– Free Meal
– $50 in “Giligin’s Bucks” (gift certificates) to be used later.
– Note: You are only allowed to win one of their challenges every other week.

title: Lindy’s “Burnout” Spicy Burger Challenge
description:
– Lindy’s on the 4th in Tucson, AZ offers 2 burger challenges (This one and a non-spicy burger challenge).
– The entire challenge weighs around 3 pounds total.

title: Venezia’s 24″ Pizza Party Challenge (Chandler)
address: 1245 S. Price Rd., Unit #3, Chandler, Arizona 85286
description:
– Venezia’s pizza has 6 locations, all based in Arizona, and all offer this challenge.
– Two people in one hour have to finish the entire 24-inch party pizza. Winners will receive a free Venezia’s shirt and their picture will be placed on our wall of fame!
– (Courtesy: Venezia’s Website)
– Note: Meal is not free or discounted

title: Cereal Killerz Kitchen’s Monster Cereal Bowl Challenge (Goodyear)
address: 13778 W. McDowell Rd, Ste #301, Goodyear, AZ 85395
weight: 80 ounces of cereal (+3/4-1 gallon of milk for "professionals")
description:
– Cereal Killerz Kitchen currently has 4 locations and all 4 of them offer this cereal challenge.
– The challenge is to finish a massive bowl filled with scoops from over 130 different cereals that Cereal Killerz Kitchen offers. The challenge bowl comes “as is” and you cannot just choose which cereals you prefer.
– The whole bowl of cereal, not including any milk, weighs around 80 ounces or 5 pounds total.
– Non-professionals get 1 hour to finish everything. There is no milk requirement for non-professionals.
– People deemed “professional” with lots of experience get only 45 minutes to finish the whole bowl, and pros must also finish either 1 full gallon of dairy milk, or 3/4 gallon of unsweetened almond milk or other non-dairy milk alternative option.
– 1 gallon of whole dairy milk or 2% dairy milk is included with the $35 fee that must be paid in advance when you order the challenge bowl online via their website. You can request a non-dairy alternative which costs an extra $5.00 on the day of your challenge.
– You must order the challenge online more than 24-48 hours in advance, and let them know your desired location, so the staff can prepare the challenge bowl ahead of time which takes more than 1 hour to prepare. CLICK HERE TO CHECK OUT THIER CHALLENGE PAGE WHERE YOU PLACE YOUR ORDER.
– Winners receive the whole cereal bowl free plus a sweet T-Shirt, a spot on the Wall of Fame, and a $50 gift card to come back and use later (good for all locations).

title: Tailgaters & Il Primo’s 30″ Pizza Challenge (Cave Creek)
where: Tailgaters Sports Grill & Il Primo Pizza & Wings (Cave Creek)
address: 16880 N Cave Creek Rd, Phoenix, AZ 85032
description:
– There are 2 different challenges that you and your team can attempt!
– 2 people have one full hour to finish a 30″ 1-topping pizza (can be a meat or a vegetable)
– 3 people have 30 minutes to finish a 30″ 4-topping pizza (3 of the toppings must be meat)
– Free Meal
– Free T-shirts
– You all get your names on a plaque
– 1 free pizza per month for 1 year (each person gets one)
– Note: The photo features the 2 person 1-topping pizza challenge

title: Tailgaters & Il Primo’s 30″ Pizza Challenge (Glendale)
where: Tailgaters Sports Grill & Il Primo Pizza & Wings (Glendale)
description:
– There are 2 different challenges that you and your team can attempt!
– 2 people have one full hour to finish a 30″ 1-topping pizza (can be a meat or a vegetable)
– 3 people have 30 minutes to finish a 30″ 4-topping pizza (3 of the toppings must be meat)
– Free Meal
– Free T-shirts
– You all get your names on a plaque
– 1 free pizza per month for 1 year (each person gets one)
– Note: The photo features the 2 person 1-topping pizza challenge

title: Tailgaters & Il Primo’s 30″ Pizza Challenge (Goodyear)
where: Tailgaters Sports Grill & Il Primo Pizza & Wings (Goodyear)
address: 530 N Estrella Pkwy suite c-5, Goodyear, AZ 85338
description:
– There are 2 different challenges that you and your team can attempt!
– 2 people have one full hour to finish a 30″ 1-topping pizza (can be a meat or a vegetable)
– 3 people have 30 minutes to finish a 30″ 4-topping pizza (3 of the toppings must be meat)
– Free Meal
– Free T-shirts
– You all get your names on a plaque
– 1 free pizza per month for 1 year (each person gets one)
– Note: The photo features the 2 person 1-topping pizza challenge

title: The Broken Yolk’s Iron Man Omelet Challenge (Yuma)
description:
–  12 egg omelet filled with mushrooms, onions, and cheese
– Smothered with their special chili and more cheese
– Includes a huge pile of homefries and 2 large biscuits
– There is a 1 hour time limit to finish everything
– Free Meal
– Free T-shirt
– Name on Special Plaque

title: The Broken Yolk’s Iron Man Omelet Challenge (Chandler)
description:
–  12 egg omelet filled with mushrooms, onions, and cheese
– Smothered with their special chili and more cheese
– Includes a huge pile of homefries and 2 large biscuits
– There is a 1 hour time limit to finish everything
– Free Meal
– Free T-shirt
– Name on Special Plaque

title: Old Times Kafe 24 Egg Omelet Challenge
CAZ Sports Bar’s Great 48oz Burger Challenge
description:
– Six 8 oz beef chuck patties on a homemade brioche bun.
– Topped with lettuce, tomatoes, onions, cheese, pickles, bacon, onion rings, and jalapeño poppers.
– Served with waffle fries, and there is a 1-hour time limit to finish everything.
– Free T-Shirt
– Wall of Fame
– Note: The meal is not free or discounted if you win.

title: Aioli Gourmet Burgers’ “Triple Threat” Eating Challenge
description:
– There is a triple-patty burger from the menu loaded with cheese, bacon, and/or healthy vegetables.
– For “threat” number two, you must finish an order of loaded fries or loaded sweet potato fries.
– The third and final “threat” is that you must finish one of Aioli’s signature milkshakes from the menu.
– There is a 15 minute time limit to finish everything you are served.
– This is normally a pay-to-play challenge, which costs $25 whether you win or lose.
– Win and get a free “I Conquered the Triple Threat Challenge” T-shirt plus Wall of Fame.
– Losers get a “The Triple Threat Challenge Conquered Me” T-shirt and go on the Wall of Shame.
– Note: There are two challenges pictured in the 2nd photo below.

title: Fat Sub’s 3 Foot Mega Sub Challenge
address: 91 London Bridge Rd, Lake Havasu City, AZ 86403
description:
– This challenge includes a 3 foot sub filled with your choice of deli meats.
– You must finish everything in one sitting to win the challenge.
– 48-hour advance notice is required.
– Free Meal
– Wall of Fame
– Free T-Shirt

title: Arizona Cardinals’ Gridiron Burger Challenge
description:
Available at Arizona Cardinals football games for the 2018 NFL season, the 7-pound Gridiron Burger includes five 1/3 pound burger patties, five all-beef hot dogs, five bratwursts, 20 slices of American cheese, eight slices of bacon, eight chicken tenders, 12 ounces of fries, lettuce, pickles and tanker sauce, all on a 10-inch bun.
If you can finish this monster by yourself in under an hour, you’ll be rewarded with an Arizona Cardinals jersey and your photo on the scoreboard.
To do the challenge, you need to order before kickoff.
– Arizona Cardinals Jersey
– In-game Scoreboard Recognition
– Note: Meal is NOT free if you finish.

title: Trident Grill’s “Navy Seal” Burger Challenge (Williams Center)
description:
– There are 4 burger patties stacked and loaded with cheese, bacon, and vegetables
– The challenge includes fries and there is a 15 minute time limit to finish everything
– Free Meal
– Wall of Fame
(Please note the photo below features TWO challenges. You don’t have to finish both.)

title: Tania’s Mighty B.Rex Burrito Challenge
description: “$14 gets you the biggest burrito we’ve ever offered stuffed with everything that’s ever dreamed of being in a burrito: beans, rice, guacamole, sour cream, pico de gallo, cheese, roasted green chiles, lettuce, potatoes, and — of course — CARNE ASADA. Filling enough for a dinosaur, but made for a human. Get your claws around one today!” If you can finish the entire burrito within 20 minutes, you win it FREE!!

description:

– Smothered with their special chili and more cheese
– Includes a huge pile of homefries and 2 large biscuits

title: Cold Beers Cheeseburger Challenge (Chandler)
description: – Starts with 3 patties and gets as large as you want! The current record as of June 2014 is 9 patties. This burger isn’t for the feint of heart, ladies and gentlemen, with each patty weighing in at half a pound. Hope you’re excited to hear that you can make a burger as big as you want. Come in to either location and try the burger challenge today. If you break the time record at any patty level, you get the meal for free.  If you eat more patties than anyone else has, you have created a new level and you get it free. (From restaurant’s website)

title: Lindy’s OMFG Burger Challenge
description:
– Lindy’s on the 4th in Tucson, AZ offers 2 burger challenges (This one and a spicy burger challenge).
– There are NINE 1/3lb burger patties stacked high and layered with cheddar and Swiss cheese.
– It is topped with lettuce, onions, tomatoes, and Lindy’s sauce (no sides included).
– The entire challenge weighs around 4 pounds total.
– Free Meal and picture on Wall of Fame if finished in less than 20 minutes
– Only picture on Wall of Fame if finished in less than 30 minutes

address: 4251 N Winfield Scott Plaza, Scottsdale, AZ 85251
description:

– Free Meal

– Note: You are only allowed to win one of their six challenges every other week.

address: 4251 N Winfield Scott Plaza, Scottsdale, AZ 85251

address: 4251 N Winfield Scott Plaza, Scottsdale, AZ 85251
description:

– On a seeded bun served with about 3.5 lbs french fries, coleslaw, and a handful of pickles
– The challenge includes a 50 oz soda and everything must be finished within 1 hour
– Free Meal
– $50 in “Giligin’s Bucks” (gift certificates)
– Note: You are only allowed to win one of their six challenges every other week.

description:
– Tom’s BBQ has 4 locations, all based in Arizona, but only the Mesa locations offer this challenge.
– Devour 3 House Sandwiches and 2 of our Fried Favorites (Onion Strings & Curly Fries) within 32 minutes.
– Are you starting to get the theme? (3 of a kind and 2 of a kind/pair).
– Each house sandwich is a 1/4 loaf (6″-8″ long) French bread filled with BBQ meat (1 pork, 1 brisket, 1 chicken), grilled onions, mushrooms, mozzarella cheese, peppers (sweet or hot giardinare), bordelaise sauce.
– Free Meal
– Free Shirt
– Picture on Wall of Fame (Losers go on Wall Of Shame)

title: Dirtwater Springs’ 72oz “Gambler’s Choice” Steak Challenge
address: 586 W Apache Trail, Apache Junction, AZ 85120
description:
Note 1: You also get a Free Shirt for winning the challenge!!
Note 2: You must give the restaurant a 24-hour notice before your attempt.
Note 3: You must put down a 50% deposit on the steak. (Not actually charged until you lose or fail to show up)

title: Fat Boyz Smash House’s “Smash Stack” Burger Challenge
description:
– This challenge consists of an 8-patty smash burger deluxe, which includes eight beef patties topped with bacon, onions, pickles, and Fat Boy sauce on a sesame seed bun.
– In addition to the burger, there is a full order of mozzarella sticks, a small order of boneless wings, 1lb of chili cheese fries, and a double order of chili cheese tots.
– There is a 30 minute time limit to finish the entire burger and sides. Successful completions win the meal for free, a sweet hat, and a photo on the wall of fame.

title: Smokin’ Joe’s Ribhouse’s “Pit Master” BBQ Challenge (Rogers)
description:
– Smoking Joe’s Ribhouse has two locations and this challenge is offered at both. This giant BBQ challenge consists of 1 full rack of baby back ribs, 1lb of pulled pork, 1lb of brisket, 3 hot links, 1/2 chicken, and a BBQ burger.
– In addition to the meats above, you also must finish a side of Mac and cheese, baked beans, fries, and rolls.
– There is a short 40 minute time limit to finish the entire platter including the sides. Successful completions win the meal for free and go up on their wall of fame.

title: Smokin’ Joe’s Ribhouse’s “Pit Master” BBQ Challenge (Bentonville)
description:
– Smoking Joe’s Ribhouse has two locations and this challenge is offered at both. This giant BBQ challenge consists of 1 full rack of baby back ribs, 1lb of pulled pork, 1lb of brisket, 3 hot links, 1/2 chicken, and a BBQ burger.
– In addition to the meats above, you also must finish a side of Mac and cheese, baked beans, fries, and rolls.
– There is a short 40 minute time limit to finish the entire platter including the sides. Successful completions win the meal for free and go up on their wall of fame.

title: Chit Chat & Chew Cafe’s “Punisher Extreme” Burger Challenge
description:
– This burger challenge consists of eight 4oz patties, each with cheese, two eggs, two hash browns, 6 slices of bacon, lettuce, and tomato all sandwiched between three burger buns.
– In addition to the giant stacked burger, there is a basket of their cheesy ranch fries and a two scoop ice cream sundae.
– There is a 40 minute time limit to finish the entire burger and sides. Winners get the meal for free, a sweet t-shirt, and a trophy!

title: Jesse James Foods LLC’s “Tombstone” Burger Challenge
address: 3000 Jennings LN suite I, Batesville, AR, United States, 72501
description:
– This burger challenge consists of two 1.5 pound burger patties, six slices of American cheese, one pound of crispy bacon, 1/4 pound of fried onion danglers, smothered in BBQ sauce and Jesse James Sauce. This is all sandwiched between a giant bun.
– In addition to the giant burger, there are 2 lbs of fries and a 44 oz milkshake.
– There is a 45 minute time limit to finish the entire platter and milkshake. Winners get the meal for free, a picture one the Winners Wall, a Jesse James Foods hat or shirt, and a feature on their social media!!

title: Las Fresas’ 32-Inch “Monster” Mexican Burrito Challenge
address: 613 N Plaza Ct, Van Buren, AR 72956
description:
– This giant 32” long burrito is stuffed with steak, chicken, pork carnitas, chorizo, French fries, rice, refried beans, sour cream, and guacamole. The entire burrito is topped with some delicious grilled shrimp.
– There are three 14″ flour burrito tortillas used to make this “Monster” Burrito Challenge.
– It comes with queso, green Salsa Verde, and red Chile Colorado sauce, and those all must be finished too.
– There is a 45 minute time limit to finish the entire burrito meal and win the challenge.
– Winners get the meal for free, a spot on the wall of champions, and a sweet t-shirt.

title: Red Oak Fillin’ Station’s “Nick” Burger Record Challenge
address: 2169 Carpenter Dam Rd, Hot Springs, AR 71913
description:
– This is a progressive record challenge. Each time someone wins, they add a layer (hamburger patty with cheese). As of 03/09/2025, the current “Nick” is eight 1/2 pound patties with cheese, so you must eat nine to break the record and win the challenge!! There is also some lettuce, bacon, and onions on the burger.
– In addition to the burger, there is a 1 pound side of loaded bacon cheese fries.
– There is a 45 minute time limit to finish the entire platter.
– Winners receive the meal for free, an “I Survived the Challenge” T-shirt, and get to rename the burger.

title: Johnny Brusco’s 6lb “Ultimate” Stromboli Challenge (Bentonville)
where: Johnny Brusco's New York Style Pizza (Bentonville)
address: 700 SE Walton Blvd, Ste #10, Bentonville, AR 72712
description:
– This “Ultimate” Stromboli Challenge features a 6 lb (2.73 kg) stromboli with all the toppings featured on Johnny’s Deluxe Pizza. It includes sausage, onions, mushrooms, green peppers, ground beef, Canadian bacon, and pepperoni. There is also a lot of shredded mozzarella cheese. There is no pizza / red marinara sauce inside the stromboli. Rather, it is served on the side in a bowl which is required to be finished as part of the challenge within the 1 hour (60 minutes) time limit.
– Fail and pay $49.99 plus tax for your meal. Winners get the entire stromboli meal free plus a sweet t-shirt and spot on the location’s Wall of Fame.
– There are many Johnny’s (in Georgia) and Johnny Brusco’s restaurants all around the southeastern United States and most if not all of them can / will offer this stromboli challenge. Find and contact the location nearest you beforehand to set up your challenge so they can be prepared and ready for your challenge attempt!!
– CLICK HERE to check out the challenge terms and conditions on Johnny Brusco’s website!!

title: This Guy’s Smoke N Grill’s 8-Patty Bacon Cheeseburger Challenge
description:
– There is an 8-patty burger and all the beef hamburger patties weigh 3 pounds total.
– The patties are stacked and layered with 1 lb of bacon strips and 8 slices of cheese.
– The challenge includes 1 lb of crinkle-cut fries and there is a 30 minute time limit.
– Winners receive the $59.99 meal for free, a sweet T-shirt, and spot on the Wall of Fame!!

title: Bucket List Cafe’s “55 Gallon Pancake” Challenge
description:

– Winners receive the $12.99 meal for free and their picture on the Wall of Fame.

description:
– This huge pizza has one topping and weighs in around 11 lbs
– You and a friend have an hour to finish the entire thing
– Free Meal
– Free T-shirt
– Spot on the Wall of Fame
– Note: You must book the challenge at least 2 days before and this challenge is only done on Sundays.

title: Bocadillos’ “Cheat Meal” Burrito Challenge
description:
– Bocadillos’ Restaurant Tacos & Ice Cream is located in Dardanelle, Arkansas.
– They’ve offered this Mexican Cheat Meal Burrito Challenge for many years.
– It weighs between 6 and 7 pounds and can be filled with any meat of your choice.
– There are scoops of refried beans plus lots of Mexican rice with healthy veggies.
– I got mine 1/2 chicken and 1/2 juicy steak plus cheeses and lots of Mexican sauces.
– There is a 45-minute time limit to finish everything. The surrounding lettuce is not required.
– Winners receive the $35 Mexican burrito meal free, a sweet t-shirt, and Wall of Fame.

title: Simple Simon’s 29″ Beast Team Pizza Challenge (De Queen)
address: 924 E Collin Raye Dr, De Queen, AR 71832
description:
The challenge features one of Simple Simon’s 29″ Beast-sized pizzas and teams of two people must pick one topping in addition to all the delicious cheese. There is a 45-minute time limit to finish the whole pizza that weighs between 9 and 10 pounds. The pizza costs around $55.99 plus tax if your team loses, but winning teams receive the meal free, sweet t-shirts, and they get their team photo posted up on the location’s Wall of Fame. If interested in trying this challenge too, please contact your nearest location to see if they too are participating and find out their rules and prizes.
– Free Meal
– T-shirts
– Wall of Fame

title: Gusano’s 30″ “Big G” Team Pizza Challenge (Fort Smith)
description:
– Gusano’s has several locations, and they all offer this challenge.
– There is a 30″ pizza covered with 3 different toppings.
– There is a 1-hour time limit for 2 people to finish it.
– Free Meal
– Free T-Shirts
– Wall of Fame

title: Brangus Steakhouse’s Free 64oz Sirloin Steak Challenge
description:
– Brangus Steakhouse in Russellville has offered their 4lb Sirloin Steak Challenge since 1995.
– Thousands of people have attempted the challenge and their have only been ~450 winners.
– There are multiple sirloin steaks that total up to weigh 4lbs or 64oz, cooked to your liking.
– The sides include a starch of your choice plus a vegetable side and slice of Texas toast.
– There is a 1 hour time limit to finish everything. Winners get the (now $79.99) meal free and Wall of Fame.

title: The Midtown Billiards Monster Burger Challenge
description:
– There is a whole can of spam with three eggs and Pepper Jack cheese on sliced bread, sandwiched between two double bacon cheeseburgers with all the fixings including jalapenos. It’s all topped with pickled okra and olives!! There is a 15-minute time limit to finish everything.
– Free Meal
– Free T-shirt
– Picture on Wall of Fame
– (Note: Challenge is only available on Sundays.)

title: Ed Walker’s 5lb Burger Challenge
description:
– Ed Walker’s Drive-In has been open since 1943 in Fort Smith, Arkansas.
– They offer a 5lb burger challenge which comes with a side item of your choice.
– There is a giant beef patty topped with cheese, condiments, and healthy veggies.
– I added bacon to my cheeseburger, but that was optional and not at all required.
– You can get a basket of any of their side items – fries, tater tots, or onions rings.
– The burger is cut into 8 pieces and there is a 45-minute time limit to finish it all.
– Winners receive the $40 meal free and a spot on Ed Walker’s Wall of Fame.

title: Grub’s Burger Patty Record Challenge (Downtown)
description:
– Grub’s provides a family friendly environment with daily specials and an amazing staff ready to make your experience not only enjoyable but memorable.
– There are other locations in Arkansas but they don’t host this burger patty record challenge.
– Grub’s serves juicy 1/2 lb beef burger patties and to win this challenge, you must break the current burger patty record in less than the 1 hour time limit with a 1/2 lb side of fries.
– You can add cheese and other toppings if interested, but they are not required. Fries are though.
– There is a 1 hour time limit to finish the entire burger plus the side of fries. Good luck!!
– Winners earn the $67 cheeseburger meal free plus a sweet t-shirt and spot on Wall of Fame!!
– As of 1/25/2022, the record is 14 half-pound beef patties (7lbs total pre-cooked). To win, you must eat 15!!

title: Mean Pig’s Shut Up Juice Sandwich Challenge
address: 3096 Bill Foster Memorial Hwy, Cabot, AR 72023
price: Apparently it costs $46.20 after taxes now?
description:
– One sandwich with ground beef and extra spicy sauce.
– There is a one sitting time limit to finish the whole sandwich, including debris.
– There is no drinking or use of extra sauces allowed during the challenge.
– Free T-Shirt
– Picture on Wall of Fame
– $40 gift card/certificate to be used later
– Note: Meal is not free or discounted if you win.

title: Smokey and the Brisket BBQ’s “Smokey-Zilla” Burger Challenge
address: 5465 Lake Murray Blvd, La Mesa, CA 91942
description:

This challenge was named after the legendary creature El Chupacabra, known for haunting various parts of the world. But at Taqueros, the Chupacabra is just a really massive enchilada! Your mission, should you choose to accept it, is to finish the whole plate with the 6 pound enchilada, as well as the beans and rice on the side.

– You cannot get up or use the restroom
– If you lose, you must pay for the enchilada
– Your enchilada for free!
– Free Taqueros Shirt

CLICK HERE TO CHECK OUT THEIR WEBSITE CHALLENGE PAGE!!

They have separate categories for men and women. The challenge for men is to eat two large (jumbo) subs (13″) in an hour, and for women one large (jumbo) sub and 1 regular (8″) sub in an hour. They keep records for both fastest times and most eaten. If you finish the challenge (2 large subs for men, or 1 large and 1 regular subs for women), you get a free t-shirt and pic on their digital Wall of Fame. The meal is not free or discounted. A large sub is around 13″ long by 6″ wide by 3″ high and they each weigh between 1.55 lbs and 1.85 lbs.
– Quantity record for men is 4 large subs in under 50 minutes.
– Quantity record for women is 3 large subs in under 50 minutes.
– The time record for men is 2 large subs in less than 9 minutes.

title: Westside’s 18″ Death By Pizza Challenge (Redding)
description:
– There are around 35 Westside Pizza locations all around the Pacific Northwest United States.
– Almost all of them offer an 18-inch “Death By Pizza” Challenge loaded with lots of tasty toppings.
– The “Death By Pizza” is their most popular specialty topping option w/ 5 meats plus a lot more!!
– Toppings: pepperoni, Canadian bacon, sausage, beef, real bacon, canned pineapple, mushrooms, black olives, onions, and green peppers.
– There is a 1 hour time limit to finish the pizza, including all the toppings. Substitutions are allowed.
– Winners receive the $27.99 pizza free (price can vary based on location), a sweet t-shirt, $50 cash, and a spot on the location’s Wall of Fame.
– If you are a location’s 1st winner or if you break the time record, you get an extra $50 cash ($100 total).
– At some locations, you can walk in and try it, but you are encouraged to schedule your attempt in advance.

title: Fat Shack’s “5K” 5,000 Calorie Menu Challenge (Wheat Ridge)
description:
There are more than 30 Fat Shack locations around the United States now, with most of them near major universities and college campuses, offering “Late Night Done Right.” Almost all of them currently offer a 5,000 Calorie “5K” Menu Eating Challenge. Challengers get just 15 minutes to quickly eat a few different items from the menu. There is a “Fat Shack” Sandwich that features cheesesteak meat, four chicken fingers, jalapeno poppers, fried mozzarella cheese sticks, French fries, and the sauces of your choosing.
The challenge also includes two orders (12) of Fat Shack’s Mac N’ Cheese Bites, a classic double cheeseburger served with fries, and five (5) deep fried Oreos. The order of fried pickle spears featured in the photo and linked video was just optional and not required as part of the actual 5,000 calorie food challenge. Winners get the $50 meal free, a sweet t-shirt of your choosing, a headband proclaiming your victory, and a spot on the Fat Shack location’s Wall of Fame. You should be able to walk in and order the challenge at most locations with no prior notice, but you may want to contact the Fat Shack fast food restaurant you are interested in visiting ahead of time so they can be prepared and ready for your attempt. Good luck, eat fast, and win before you begin!!

– This is a challenge with 2lbs of beef and 2lbs of noodles.

description:
– Beau Jo’s Pizza has several locations, all based in CO, but only a couple locations offer this challenge.

– The depth of this pizza is over 3″ tall

where: Belvedere Sandwich Shop and Polish Deli
address: 122 Prospect Hill Rd, East Windsor, CT 06088
description: There is a “mountain” of Polish foods, all weighing about 5 lbs / 2.28 kg total. There are 5 potato pancakes, 24 potato & cheese pierogies, 5 pieces of kielbasa, 2 scoops of Kapusta (sauerkraut), and 1 stuffed cabbage roll topped with a red sauce. There is a 30 minute time limit to finish the entire Polish food platter. Winners get the $49 meal free plus a sweet T-shirt and spot on Belvedere’s Wall of Fame!!

title: Ferris Acres Creamery’s “Ol’ Creamery” Ice Cream Sundae Challenge
description:
– This challenge consists of a giant ice cream sundae composed of 12 scoops of ice cream (your choice, 6 flavors – 2 scoops of each) and 6 toppings of your choice. It is served in a half gallon bucket and topped with whipped cream.
– There is a one sitting time limit to finish the entire ice cream sundae. Teams are welcome to complete the challenge, but only solo contenders who finish on the premises will be awarded a sweet t-shirt for completion.
– NOTE: The meal is NOT free or discounted upon completion.

title: Sweet Ashley’s Ice Cream’s “Kitchen Sink” Ice Cream Sundae Challenge
description:
– This challenge consists of 12 large scoops of ice cream (4 vanilla, 4 chocolate, 4 strawberry), 2 brownies, bananas, chocolate fudge, caramel, strawberries, whip cream, sprinkles, cherries and optional nuts.
– There is a short 15 minute time limit to complete the entire ice cream sundae. Winners get the ice cream for free, a sweet Ashley’s t-shirt, and photo on Ashley’s wall of fame, and a $25 gift card.
– NOTE – The time limit was changed from 30 minutes to 15 minutes recently.

title: Clevelands’ Country Store’s “Godfather” Ice Cream Sundae Challenge
description:

– There is a 30 minute time limit to complete the entire ice cream sundae. Winners get the ice cream for free, go up on the wall of fame, and get to keep the Radio Flyer wagon! (How cool!)

title: Scoopy’s Ice Cream’s “Mega Scoop” Ice Cream Sundae Challenge
description:
– This challenge consists of 14 scoops of ice cream of your choice, 6 toppings of your choice, 6 cherries, 2 sauces of your choice, and whipped cream.
– There is a short 14 minutes to complete the entire ice cream sundae. Winners get the ice cream for free, a sweet t-shirt, and picture up on Scoopy’s wall of fame.

title: Thomas Hooker Brewery’s “Big Hooker” Burger Challenge
description:
– Thomas Hooker Brewery has two eating challenges. The first is their 16-inch diameter “Big Hooker” Pizza Challenge and the second is their “Big Hooker” Burger Challenge.
– Both food challenges are only offered at the original Bloomfield, Connecticut brewery location!!
– The burger challenge consists of two triple patty “Whippers,” which each have 3 cheeseburger patties.
– In addition to the burgers, there is 1 lb of tater tots, 1 lb of chicken tenders, and a 16 oz beer or seltzer.
– There is a short 25 minute time limit to finish the entire burger platter and beverage. Winners receive the $40 burger meal free, a beverage on the house, a $25 gift card, a sweet t-shirt, and picture on Wall of Fame!!

title: Thomas Hooker Brewery’s “Big Hooker” Pizza Challenge
description:

There is a double patty Philly Burger with Certified Angus Beef, a Philly pretzel bun, cheese whiz, fried onions, and jalapeno peppers. Third, there is a single patty Village Burger with a Certified Angus Beef hamburger patty topped with goat cheese, mushrooms, bacon, lettuce, and tomato. Challengers must also finish a flavored milkshake of their choosing.
– Winners receive the $35 cheeseburger meal free plus a sweet T-Shirt and spot on the Wall of Fame.

title: 3-D Eats Food Truck’s “3-D Defcon” Melt Sandwich Challenge
description:
– The “3-D Defcon” melt sandwich challenge consists of five of their menu sandwiches, all stacked together to make one tall sandwich, or “levels”.
– The challenge sandwich consists of:
– In addition to the stacked sandwich, there is a 1lb side of fries and a 16oz 3-D Tea or lemonade that is required.
– The challenge must be completed in level order, from Level 5 to Level 1, in order, without mixing any layers.
– There is a short 25 minute time limit to finish the entire challenge. Winners get the meal for free or a American Silver dollar 2020 Philadelphia minting coin, a survivor “Teashirt”, the privilege to sound the air raid siren, and immortality on the wall of heroes.
-NOTE: This is a mobile food truck. If interested in trying the challenge, please contact them ahead of time to figure out where they will be stationed and to set up your attempt!

title: The Bun Lab PCB’s “Big Byte” Burger Challenge
address: 9902 S Thomas Dr #13, Panama City Beach, FL 32408
description:
– This giant burger challenge starts with two 1 pound juicy beef smash patties, which are loaded with American cheese, tomato, lettuce, onion, pickle and secret sauce. This is all served on a 9 inch oversized burger bun.
– In addition to the giant burger, there is a 1/2lb side of fries and a 20oz. soda.
– There is a short 20 minute time limit to finish the entire burger, fries, and drink. Winners get the meal for free, a sweet t-shirt to show off your victory, and a spot on the wall of fame.

title: Downtown Diner’s “5 Footlong” Hot Dog Challenge
description:
– The “5 Footlong” Hot Dog Challenge consist of five footlong hot dogs and buns. Four of the hot dogs must have at least one topping on them. Toppings choices include: cheese sauce, chili, sauerkraut, or onions.
– In addition to the hot dogs, the challenger must finish five servings of fries.
– There is a short 20 minute time limit to finish the all the hot dogs and fries. Successful completions win the meal for free, $75 cash, and a picture with the “Title Belt”, which will be put on the wall of fame.

title: Dick’s Last Resort’s “Big Meat” Steak Challenge (Orlando)
description:
– There are Dick’s Last Resort locations all around the country, and they all offer this “Big Meat” Challenge.
– The main feature of this challenge is two 12 oz New York strip steaks that are cooked to your liking.
– The steaks are the main feature of the challenge, but they aren’t really the hardest part to finish.
– Along with the steak, you must finish an order of their Macho Nachos which weighs over 3 lbs.
– The nachos are topped with cheese, homemade chili, Pico de Gallo, jalapenos, and sour cream.
– Another major feature of this challenge is Dick’s huge 12-inch footlong wiener hot dog loaded with chili, cheese, and onions. You must also finish a house salad, a double portion of their loaded red skin mashed potatoes (taters), and a double order of steamed broccoli. Good luck and win before you begin!!
– Winners receive the $79.99 meal free, a sweet t-shirt, and a spot on the location’s Wall of Fame.
– Please note that the drinks and dessert featured in the video below were just optional and not required.

title: Fresh Jalapeno Mexican Restaurant’s 10lb Team Burrito Challenge
description:
– This challenge consists of a giant, 24-inch, 10-pound burrito which is stuffed with ground beef, shredded beef, shredded chicken, rice, beans, guacamole, pico de Gallo, and sour cream. The burrito is topped with lettuce and cheese dip. Altercations may be made if and only if a challenger is allergic to one of the ingredients.
– In addition to the burrito, a beverage is also required for each person. This beverage can be a fountain drink or a house margarita (which costs an additional $9.99).
– Teams of two people have a 40-minute time limit to finish the entire burrito and beverages. Successful completions win the meal for free, a Fresh Jalapeño Challenge t-shirt, and photo added to the wall of fame.
– No bathroom breaks are allowed once the timer starts. There is no sharing or help from others besides your teammate, and no hiding food. If you do receive help, you will be disqualified and charged a $30 fee. A “reversal of fortune” will also result in a disqualification and loss of the challenge.
– NOTE: This challenge may also be tried as a solo challenge, but the time limit is increased to 60 minutes.

title: Fresh Jalapeno Mexican Restaurant’s 10lb Solo Burrito Challenge
description:

title: Fat Shack’s “5K” 5,000 Calorie Menu Eating Challenge (Orlando)
description:
There are more than 30 Fat Shack locations around the United States now, with most of them near major universities and college campuses, offering “Late Night Done Right.” Almost all of them currently offer a 5,000 Calorie “5K” Menu Eating Challenge. Challengers get just 15 minutes to quickly eat a few different items from the menu. There is a “Fat Shack” Sandwich that features cheesesteak meat, four chicken fingers, jalapeno poppers, fried mozzarella cheese sticks, French fries, and the sauces of your choosing.
The challenge also includes two orders (12) of Fat Shack’s Mac N’ Cheese Bites, a classic double cheeseburger served with fries, and five (5) deep fried Oreos. The order of fried pickle spears featured in the photo and linked video was just optional and not required as part of the actual 5,000 calorie food challenge. Winners get the $50 meal free, a sweet t-shirt of your choosing, a headband proclaiming your victory, and a spot on the Fat Shack location’s Wall of Fame. You should be able to walk in and order the challenge at most locations with no prior notice, but you may want to contact the Fat Shack fast food restaurant you are interested in visiting ahead of time so they can be prepared and ready for your attempt. Good luck, eat fast, and win before you begin!!

where: The New York Bakery Boys (Bronson Memorial Hwy)

6117 West Irlo Bronson Memorial Hwy,
Celebration, FL 34747
description:
– This challenge consists of 13 (baker’s dozen) bagels and cream cheese.
– There is a 30 minute time limit for professional eaters and 60 minute time limit for amateurs. Winners get the meal for free, a custom t-shirt, photo on the wall of fame, and the progressive jackpot!
– All food must be swallowed within the time limit to count as a win. Water or other provided beverages are allowed but excessive use of soaking food (dunking) is prohibited.
– Plates, bowls, and the table must be clean of food to count as a win. There is also a 5 minute waiting period after the challenge is over to ensure there is no “reversal of fortune” before a win is declared.
